title: TaRL Africa Is Hiring: Country Regional Manager (Programs) – Kaduna, Nigeria | Apply by 3 March 2026
summary: TaRL Africa is expanding its leadership team in Nigeria and is now hiring a Country Regional Manager (Programs) to drive regional scale, government partnership, and high-quality implementation of the Teaching at the Right Level (TaRL) approach. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); If you are an experienced program leader with a strong background in education systems reform, government engagement, and multi-state program management, this is an opportunity to lead transformative change at scale. Position: Country Regional Manager, Programs Location: Kaduna, Nigeria Reports to: Country Director, TaRL Africa Nigeria About TaRL Africa TaRL Africa is hosted by Empower Learning Africa, a not-for-profit organization headquartered in Nairobi, Kenya, with teams across C te d Ivoire, Nigeria, and Zambia. The initiative was launched in 2019 as a joint venture between Pratham and J- PAL. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); TaRL Africa partners with governments and organizations across more than 15 Sub-Saharan African countries to accelerate children s foundational skill acquisition using the evidence-based Teaching at the Right Level methodology. Together with partners, the initiative has reached over five million children. Our vision is for every child across Africa to have strong foundational skills that unlock lifelong opportunity. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the Role: Country Regional Manager (CRM) The Country Regional Manager (CRM) will lead and expand TaRL programming within a defined region in Nigeria. This role oversees implementation across multiple states, manages regional teams, strengthens partnerships with state governments, and ensures program delivery with fidelity, scale, and sustainability. As a key member of the Country Management Team, you will shape regional strategy, drive operational excellence, oversee budgets, and lead continuous program improvement. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); This position requires extensive travel within the region and occasional travel across Nigeria and Africa. Key Responsibilities 1. Program Leadership Partnership Management (40%) Strengthen technical implementation of the Teaching at the Right Level model. Ensure high-quality program delivery through data-informed decision-making. Provide oversight to state teams to maintain financial health and operational efficiency. Guide annual planning cycles and conduct periodic program reviews. Build strong, productive relationships with government stakeholders and community partners. Drive contextual program design aligned with regional needs. 2. Regional Strategy Scale (30%) Identify and execute strategic expansion opportunities across states. Promote government ownership and long-term sustainability. Translate regional priorities into coordinated work plans and budgets. Use needs assessments, stakeholder insights, and program data to refine strategy. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); 3. Strengthening the TaRL Ecosystem (15%) Improve operational tools, systems, and accountability frameworks. Lead special innovation projects in collaboration with Programme Design, Pedagogy, and Monitoring, Learning Evaluation teams. Contribute to cross-state learning and organizational knowledge sharing. 4. Team Leadership Performance Management (15%) Build, mentor, and manage high-performing regional teams. Set clear performance goals and monitor progress. Foster accountability, ownership, and continuous professional development. Cultivate a collaborative and impact-driven team culture. Who We Are Looking For We are seeking a results-oriented leader with: Proven experience managing medium to large-scale education or development programs Strong stakeholder engagement experience with governments and donors Demonstrated ability to lead multi-state implementation Strategic planning and expansion expertise Budget oversight and financial management capability Strong analytical and data-driven decision-making skills Excellent written and verbal communication skills ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Minimum Qualifications Bachelor s degree in Public Policy, International Development, Education, or related field (Master s degree such as MPA or MPP preferred). At least six (6) years of experience managing medium to large-scale programs. Experience working with donors, policymakers, researchers, and government agencies. Strong proficiency in Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and Google Suite. Excellent communication skills. Why Join TaRL Africa? This is more than a job. It is a leadership opportunity to shape foundational learning outcomes for millions of children across Nigeria. You will: Influence education reform at scale Strengthen government systems for sustainable impact Lead high-performing teams across multiple states Contribute to a growing, mission-driven organization operating across Africa Play a key role in evidence-based education transformation ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); If you are ready to lead with purpose and deliver measurable impact, we encourage you to apply. Application Deadline: 3rd March 2026 APPLY HERE For more information on this job opportunity, click here . For more opportunities on jobs , click here . The post TaRL Africa Is Hiring: Country Regional Manager (Programs) – Kaduna, Nigeria | Apply by 3 March 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Consilium Traineeships at the Council of the European Union 2026–2027: Paid EU Internship Opportunities in Brussels
summary: Are you looking for a paid European Union traineeship that offers real experience inside EU institutions? The Consilium Traineeship Programme at the Council of the European Union provides young professionals with the opportunity to work directly with EU policy experts, gain institutional experience, and contribute to high-level decision-making processes. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); Applications are now open for the upcoming traineeship cycle a highly competitive programme designed for ambitious graduates interested in European affairs, governance, law, policy, and international cooperation. What Are Consilium Traineeships? The Consilium traineeship programme offers first-hand exposure to how the Council of the European Union and its General Secretariat operate. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The name Consilium comes from the Latin word meaning counsel or deliberation , reflecting the Council s role in shaping EU decisions and supporting member states in policy coordination. As a trainee, you will: Gain practical experience within EU institutions Work alongside experienced advisors and policy professionals Contribute to the Council s daily activities Develop professional skills in an international environment Build networks across European institutions ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); The programme is ideal for graduates interested in EU governance, policy-making, international relations, law, public administration, or related fields. Traineeships are paid and include allowances and other benefits. Around 110 traineeship positions are available each year. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Your traineeship: tasks, grants, allowances Paid Traineeship Benefits The Consilium traineeships are fully paid and include financial support designed to help trainees live and work in Brussels. Key benefits include: Paid monthly grant Additional allowances and benefits Professional training and mentoring Exposure to real EU policy work International multicultural working environment Approximately 110 traineeship positions are offered each year, making it one of the most attractive EU traineeship opportunities for early-career professionals. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Current Application Window (2026 Cycle) Applications are currently open for the next traineeship term. title: Inter-American Development Bank (IDB) Internship Program 2026: Paid Global Internships for Students & Recent Graduates | Complete Guide
summary: The Inter-American Development Bank (IDB) Internship Program offers a unique opportunity for students and recent graduates to gain hands-on experience in international development, finance, research, policy, and innovation. Through paid internships at IDB, IDB Invest, and IDB Lab , participants work alongside global experts and contribute to projects that improve lives across Latin America, the Caribbean, Asia, and Europe. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); If you are looking to launch your international career with a respected multilateral organization, this guide explains everything you need to know from eligibility requirements to application tips. Why Choose the IDB Internship Program? The IDB internship program is designed to help students and young professionals: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Gain practical experience in international development Work on real projects with global impact Build professional networks across regions Learn about development finance and policy Experience the organizational culture of the IDB Group Interns work directly with teams at IDB Headquarters in Washington, D.C., or in country offices worldwide, with occasional teleworking flexibility depending on the role. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Types of Internship Opportunities 1. Internships at IDB Headquarters (Washington, D.C.) Interns at headquarters support teams delivering operational and corporate solutions to public and private sector clients. Program Structure Duration: 2 months Work schedule: Full-time (40 hours per week) Format: On-site with some flexibility ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Internship Cycles Winter Internship: January 16 March 15 Summer Internship: June 16 August 15 Applications typically open 3 4 months in advance . Important Note: Applicants may apply for a maximum of three internship positions . 2. Internships at Country Offices (COFs) The IDB has offices across Latin America and the Caribbean, as well as Asia and Europe. These internships are designed to strengthen local development capacity while helping interns build careers in their own regions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Key Features Duration: 2 to 6 months (based on business needs) No fixed cohort timeline Continuous recruitment throughout the year Eligibility Requirement You must be: A citizen or legal resident of the country where the office is located, or Hold a valid work permit and reside in that country. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Fields of Study in Demand The IDB welcomes students from diverse academic backgrounds, including: Business and Finance Communications and Marketing Data and Technology Economics and Research Environmental Studies Humanities and Social Sciences Law and Policy Linguistic Services Basic Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the IDB Internship Program, applicants must meet the following criteria: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Citizenship Washington, D.C. internships: Must be a citizen of one of IDB s 48 member countries Country Office internships: Must be a citizen or legal resident of the country where applying IDB Invest roles: No citizenship restrictions ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Academic Status Active undergraduate student (minimum second year), or Graduate student with at least 50% of coursework completed Availability Must be available to work full-time (40 hours per week) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Additional Requirements Must not have relatives working at IDB, IDB Invest, or IDB Lab Internship must be your first job within the IDB Group ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Required Documents Applicants must prepare the following documents: Updated Resume (CV) University Enrollment Letter (issued within the last 3 months) Academic Transcripts (official or unofficial; issued within the last 3 months) Note: If you advance in the recruitment process, official transcripts may be required. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); How to Apply for IDB Internships Step 1: Online Application Complete the application form and upload your CV in English or Spanish. A cover letter is only required if specified in the job posting. Pro Tips Read the job description carefully Tailor your CV to match the role Keep your resume concise and results-focused Step 2: Assessment Interview If shortlisted, you may be invited to: Complete a technical assessment Attend a formal panel interview with IDB staff ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Pro Tips Follow instructions carefully during assessments Practice interview responses beforehand Be clear, authentic, and concise in your answers Step 3: Final Decision Recruitment timelines may vary. The IDB provides estimated timelines and applicants can follow up with HR for updates. If you are not selected, you are encouraged to apply again as new positions open regularly. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Visa Information for International Applicants For internships at headquarters in Washington, D.C., the IDB supports eligible candidates with G4 Visa processing . Key points: Available to non-U.S. citizens and non-permanent residents Eligible dependents may also receive G4 visas Dependents may apply for U.S. work authorization Employees must live within 50 miles of headquarters Visa approval remains at the discretion of the U.S. Department of State. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(139); }); What It s Like to Be an IDB Intern Interns often highlight: Exposure to international development projects Networking with global teams Opportunities to contribute to regional impact Professional growth and career clarity Many interns describe the program as a stepping stone into careers in international organizations, development finance, and policy. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) How long does the application process take? The process may take several weeks or months depending on the role. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(140); }); Do I need a cover letter? Only if requested in the specific job posting. Does the IDB hire globally? Yes. The IDB recruits for headquarters and country offices worldwide. Are there eligibility restrictions? Yes. Citizenship requirements apply for most positions, except some roles at IDB Invest. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(141); }); The IDB Internship Program is a strong opportunity for students and recent graduates who want to build careers in international development, economics, finance, and public policy. With paid placements, global exposure, and meaningful project work, the program provides a solid foundation for future leadership in development. If you are serious about building a global career, prepare your documents early, tailor your CV carefully, and apply strategically. Apply Learn More For full program details and available internship opportunities, visit the official IDB careers platform and explore open positions regularly. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(142); }); For a detailed list of available positions and Application, click HERE . Apply Now Don t miss this opportunity to gain hands-on experience in international development. Visit our official website to submit your application before the deadline. For further inquiries or accommodations, contact us at diversity@iadb.org .
